subject: SimuVEX
truth: Stop using SimuVEX; its components have been integrated into angr directly.
quote: SimuVEX has been removed and its components have been integrated into angr

subject: simuvex.SimProcedure
truth: Replace all imports of simuvex.SimProcedure with angr.SimProcedure.
quote: simuvex.SimProcedure -> angr.SimProcedure

subject: simuvex.SimEngine
truth: Replace all imports of simuvex.SimEngine with angr.SimEngine.
quote: simuvex.SimEngine -> angr.SimEngine

subject: simuvex.SimCC
truth: Replace all imports of simuvex.SimCC with angr.SimCC.
quote: simuvex.SimCC -> angr.SimCC

subject: simuvex.s_cc module
truth: Replace imports of simuvex.s_cc with angr.calling_conventions.
quote: simuvex.s_cc -> angr.calling_conventions

subject: simuvex.s_state module
truth: Replace imports of simuvex.s_state with angr.sim_state.
quote: simuvex.s_state -> angr.sim_state

subject: simuvex.s_procedure module
truth: Replace imports of simuvex.s_procedure with angr.sim_procedure.
quote: simuvex.s_procedure -> angr.sim_procedure

subject: simuvex.plugins module
truth: Replace imports of simuvex.plugins with angr.state_plugins.
quote: simuvex.plugins -> angr.state_plugins

subject: simuvex.concretization_strategies module
truth: Replace imports of simuvex.concretization_strategies with angr.concretization_strategies.
quote: simuvex.concretization_strategies -> angr.concretization_strategies

subject: simuvex.SimProcedures (global variable)
truth: Replace references to simuvex.SimProcedures with angr.SIM_PROCEDURES (note the rename from class-style to global variable).
quote: simuvex.SimProcedures has been renamed to angr.SIM_PROCEDURES, since it is a global variable and not a class.

subject: Path
truth: Stop using Path; its components have been integrated into SimState, notably the new history state plugin.
quote: Path has been removed and its components have been integrated into SimState, notably the new history state plugin

subject: path.trace accessor
truth: Replace path.trace with state.history.descriptions.
quote: path.trace -> state.history.descriptions

subject: path.addr_trace accessor
truth: Replace path.addr_trace with state.history.bbl_addrs.
quote: path.addr_trace -> state.history.bbl_addrs

subject: path.jumpkinds accessor
truth: Replace path.jumpkinds with state.history.jumpkinds.
quote: path.jumpkinds -> state.history.jumpkinds

subject: path.guards accessor
truth: Replace path.guards with state.history.jump_guards.
quote: path.guards -> state.history.jump_guards

subject: path.targets accessor
truth: Replace path.targets with state.history.jump_targets.
quote: path.targets -> state.history.jump_targets

subject: path.recent_actions
truth: Stop using path.recent_actions; use state.history.recent_actions instead.
quote:
path.recent_actions

state.history.recent_actions

subject: path actions tracking
old: actions tracked by default on paths
new: actions no longer tracked by default; add angr.options.refs to state to re-enable
truth: Actions are no longer tracked by default; add angr.options.refs to your state to re-enable tracking.
quote: An important behavior change about path.actions and path.recent_actions - actions are no longer tracked by default. If you would like them to be tracked again, please add angr.options.refs to your state.

from_fact: actions tracked by default on paths
actions no longer tracked by default; add angr.options.refs to state to re-enable

subject: PathGroup
truth: Stop using PathGroup; use SimulationManager (abbreviated simgr) instead, created via project.factory.simulation_manager().
quote:
Path Group -> Simulation Manager

Since there are no paths, there cannot be a path group. Instead, we have a Simulation Manager now (we recommend using the abbreviation "simgr" in places you were previously using "pg"), which is exactly the same as a path group except it holds states instead of paths. You can make one with project.factory.simulation_manager(...).

subject: errored path handling
old: ErroredPath subclass of Path placed in errored stash of path group
new: ErrorRecord object (not a SimState subclass) placed in simgr.errored list (not a stash)
truth: Error resilience is now at the simulation manager level; errors produce ErrorRecord objects (with .state, .error, .traceback, .debug()) placed in simgr.errored list, not ErroredPath subclasses in a stash.
quote: Before, error resilience was handled at the path level, where stepping a path that caused an error would return a subclass of Path called ErroredPath, and these paths would be put in the errored stash of a path group. Now, error resilience is handled at the simulation manager level, and any state that throws an error during stepping will be wrapped in an ErrorRecord object, which is not a subclass of SimState, and put into the errored list attribute of the simulation manager, which is not a stash.

from_fact: erroredpath subclass of path placed in errored stash of path group
errorrecord object (not a simstate subclass) placed in simgr.errored list (not a stash)

subject: SimProcedures catalog organization
old: SimProcedures organized strictly by library (e.g. SimProcedures['libc.so.6'])
new: SimProcedures organized by standard (e.g. SIM_PROCEDURES['libc'], SIM_PROCEDURES['posix'], SIM_PROCEDURES['glibc'])
truth: SimProcedures are no longer organized by library name; they are now organized by standard (e.g. SIM_PROCEDURES['libc'], SIM_PROCEDURES['posix'], SIM_PROCEDURES['glibc']).
quote: the old SimProcedures['libc.so.6'] has been split up between SIM_PROCEDURES['libc'], SIM_PROCEDURES['posix'], and SIM_PROCEDURES['glibc'], depending on what specifications each function conforms to.

from_fact: simprocedures organized strictly by library (e.g. simprocedures['libc.so.6'])
simprocedures organized by standard (e.g. sim_procedures['libc'], sim_procedures['posix'], sim_procedures['glibc'])

subject: overriding default SimProcedures in dict
truth: Stop overriding SimProcedures dict entries to change default hooks; use SIM_LIBRARIES[lib].add(func_name, proc) or hook_symbol instead.
quote: if you previously used a trick where you changed one of the SimProcedures present in the SimProcedures dict in order to change which SimProcedures would be used to hook over library functions by default, this will no longer work. Instead of SimProcedures[lib][func_name] = proc, you now need to say SIM_LIBRARIES[lib].add(func_name, proc). But really you should just be using hook_symbol anyway.

subject: Hook class
truth: Stop using the Hook class; use SimProcedure instances or plain functions instead.
quote: The Hook class is gone.

subject: project.hook() signature
truth: project.hook now takes an instance of a SimProcedure (call proc(...)) or a plain function, not a class.
quote: previously, where you would have done project.hook(addr, Hook(proc, ...)) or project.hook(addr, proc), you can now do project.hook(addr, proc(...)).

subject: IS_FUNCTION flag on SimProcedure
truth: Stop setting IS_FUNCTION = True on SimProcedure when using self.call(); it is no longer required.
quote: It is no longer required to set IS_FUNCTION = True if you intend to use self.call() while writing a SimProcedure

subject: hook_symbol behavior
old: hook_symbol redoes relocations for the symbol
new: hook_symbol hooks directly over the symbol address without redoing relocations
truth: hook_symbol no longer redoes relocations; it hooks directly over the symbol's address in whatever library it comes from.
quote: The hook_symbol method will no longer attempt to redo relocations for the given symbol, instead just hooking directly over the address of the symbol in whatever library it comes from.

from_fact: hook_symbol redoes relocations for the symbol
hook_symbol hooks directly over the symbol address without redoing relocations

subject: project._extern_obj
truth: Replace project._extern_obj with loader.extern_object.
quote: project._extern_obj -> loader.extern_object

subject: project._syscall_obj
truth: Replace project._syscall_obj with loader.kernel_object.
quote: project._syscall_obj -> loader.kernel_object

subject: loader.whats_at()
truth: Replace loader.whats_at() with loader.describe_addr.
quote: loader.whats_at() -> loader.describe_addr

subject: loader.addr_belongs_to_object()
truth: Replace loader.addr_belongs_to_object() with loader.find_object_containing().
quote: loader.addr_belongs_to_object() -> loader.find_object_containing()

subject: loader.find_symbol_name()
truth: Replace loader.find_symbol_name() with loader.find_symbol().name.
quote: loader.find_symbol_name() -> loader.find_symbol().name

subject: loader.find_symbol() usage
old: whatever you were doing before to look up a symbol
truth: Use loader.find_symbol(name or addr) as the unified way to look up a symbol.
quote: whatever the hell you were doing before to look up a symbol -> loader.find_symbol(name or addr)

from_fact: whatever you were doing before to look up a symbol

subject: loader.find_module_name()
truth: Replace loader.find_module_name() with loader.find_object_containing().provides.
quote: loader.find_module_name() -> loader.find_object_containing().provides

subject: loader.find_symbol_got_entry()
truth: Replace loader.find_symbol_got_entry() with loader.find_relevant_relocations().
quote: loader.find_symbol_got_entry() -> loader.find_relevant_relocations()

subject: loader.main_bin
truth: Replace loader.main_bin with loader.main_object.
quote: loader.main_bin -> loader.main_object

subject: anything.get_min_addr()
truth: Replace .get_min_addr() with .min_addr (property instead of method).
quote: anything.get_min_addr() -> anything.min_addr

subject: symbol.addr
truth: Replace symbol.addr with symbol.linked_addr.
quote: symbol.addr -> symbol.linked_addr

subject: state.BV and state.BVV
truth: Stop using state.BV and state.BVV, use state.se.BVS and state.se.BVV instead.
quote: state.BV and state.BVV are deprecated. Please use state.se.BVS and state.se.BVV.

subject: cast_to=str in solver methods
truth: Replace cast_to=str with cast_to=bytes when evaluating solver expressions to get byte representations.
quote: where you previously said state.solver.eval(x, cast_to=str) you should now say cast_to=bytes

subject: string literals in solver comparisons
old: plain string literals (unicode) in solver comparisons
truth: When creating concrete bitvectors from strings (including comparisons against a string), use bytestrings (b'...') not unicode strings, or a warning will be printed.
quote: When creating concrete bitvectors from strings (including implicitly by just making a comparison against a string) these should be bytestrings. If they are not they will be utf-8 converted and a warning will be printed.

from_fact: plain string literals (unicode) in solver comparisons

subject: memory.read_bytes return type
truth: CLE memory.read_bytes now returns bytes instead of a list of strings.
quote: memory.read_bytes(addr, n) -> list[str]

subject: memory.load
new: memory.load(addr, n) -> bytes (no change in signature, but context implies changes in underlying behavior)
truth: Stop using memory.load; use memory.store and memory.read_bytes instead.
quote:
memory.load(addr, n) -> bytes

memory.write_bytes(addr, list[str])

memory.store(addr, bytes)

memory.get_byte(addr) -> str

memory[addr] -> int

memory.read_addr_at(addr) -> int

memory.unpack_word(addr) -> int

memory.write_addr_at(addr, value) -> int

memory.pack_word(addr, value)

memory.stride_repr -> list[(start, end, str)]

memory.backers() -> iter[(start, bytearray)]

from_fact: memory.load(addr, n) -> bytes (no change in signature, but context implies changes in underlying behavior)

subject: memory.write_bytes
truth: Stop using memory.write_bytes with list[str]; use memory.store(addr, bytes) instead.
quote:
memory.write_bytes(addr, list[str])

memory.store(addr, bytes)

subject: memory.get_byte
truth: Stop using memory.get_byte which returned a str; use memory[addr] which returns an int instead.
quote:
memory.get_byte(addr) -> str

memory[addr] -> int

subject: memory.read_addr_at
truth: Stop using memory.read_addr_at; use memory.unpack_word instead.
quote:
memory.read_addr_at(addr) -> int

memory.unpack_word(addr) -> int

subject: memory.write_addr_at
truth: Stop using memory.write_addr_at; use memory.pack_word instead.
quote:
memory.write_addr_at(addr, value) -> int

memory.pack_word(addr, value)

subject: memory.pack_word and memory.unpack_word
old: pack_word(addr, value) and unpack_word(addr) with no extra parameters
new: pack_word and unpack_word now take optional size, endness, and signed parameters
truth: pack_word and unpack_word now accept optional size, endness, and signed parameters; existing calls without these parameters still work but behavior may differ if defaults changed.
quote: Additionally, pack_word and unpack_word now take optional size, endness, and signed parameters.

from_fact: pack_word(addr, value) and unpack_word(addr) with no extra parameters
pack_word and unpack_word now take optional size, endness, and signed parameters

subject: cbackers and read_bytes_c functions
truth: Stop using cbackers and read_bytes_c functions; they have been removed. Use memory.backers() which now returns bytearrays directly.
quote: If you were using the cbackers or read_bytes_c functions, the conversion is a little more complicated - we were able to remove the split notion of "backers" and "updates" and replaced all backers with bytearrays that we mutate, so we can work directly with the backer objects.

subject: memory.backers()
old: memory.backers() -> iter[(start, bytearray)] (no optional address parameter)
new: memory.backers() now accepts an optional address parameter to skip backers ending before that address
truth: memory.backers() now accepts an optional address parameter that skips backers ending before that address.
quote: The backers() function iterates through all bottom-level backer objects and their start addresses. You can provide an optional address to the function, and it will skip over all backers that end before that address.

from_fact: memory.backers() -> iter[(start, bytearray)] (no optional address parameter)
memory.backers() now accepts an optional address parameter to skip backers ending before that address

subject: object.symbols_by_addr
truth: Stop using object.symbols_by_addr; use object.symbols (a sorted list of Symbol objects) instead.
quote: symbols_by_addr is deprecated and here is now object.symbols, a sorted list of Symbol objects, to enumerate symbols in a binary.

subject: custom_* parameters in CLE
new: base_addr, entry_point, offset, arch, ld_path (without custom_ prefix)
truth: Stop using custom_base_addr, custom_entry_point, custom_offset, custom_arch, custom_ld_path; use base_addr, entry_point, offset, arch, ld_path instead.
quote: All parameters in cle that started with custom_ - so, custom_base_addr, custom_entry_point, custom_offset, custom_arch, and custom_ld_path - have had the custom_ removed from the beginning of their names.

from_fact: base_addr, entry_point, offset, arch, ld_path (without custom_ prefix)

subject: functions deprecated before angr 7 release
old: All functions deprecated at or before the angr 7 release
truth: All functions that were deprecated at or before the angr 7 release have been removed.
quote: All the functions that were deprecated more than a year ago (at or before the angr 7 release) have been removed.

from_fact: all functions deprecated at or before the angr 7 release

subject: immutable simulation managers
truth: Support for immutable simulation managers has been removed.
quote:
Support for immutable simulation managers has been removed.
So far as we're aware, nobody was actually using this, and it was making debugging a pain.

subject: SimCC customization
old: SimCC could be customized via sp_delta, args, or ret_val parameters
new: SimCC can no longer be customized with sp_delta, args, or ret_val; use SimCCUsercall instead
truth: Stop using sp_delta, args, or ret_val parameters on SimCC; use SimCCUsercall which requires explicit argument locations.
quote:
SimCCs can no longer be customized

If you were using the sp_delta, args, or ret_val parameters to SimCC, you should use the new class
SimCCUsercall, which lets (requires) you to be explicit about the locations of each argument.

from_fact: simcc could be customized via sp_delta, args, or ret_val parameters
simcc can no longer be customized with sp_delta, args, or ret_val; use simccusercall instead

subject: SimTypes requirement
truth: Passing SimTypes is now mandatory when using SimCC.
quote: Passing SimTypes is now mandatory

subject: SimCC method calls
truth: Stop passing is_fp and size to SimCC methods; pass a SimType instead.
quote: Every method call on SimCC which interacts with typed data now requires a SimType to be passed in. Previously, the use of is_fp and size was optional, but now these parameters will no longer be accepted and a SimType will be required.

associative: pass type info to calling convention

subject: call_state, callable, SimProcedure.call()
truth: call_state, callable, and SimProcedure.call() now require a function prototype to be passed.
quote: Additionally, some non-cc interfaces, such as call_state and callable and SimProcedure.call(), now require a prototype to be passed to them.

subject: PointerWrapper
truth: PointerWrapper now has a buffer argument; pass buffer=True to treat the wrapped value as an array of the child type instead of a scalar.
quote: The buffer argument to PointerWrapper now instructs SimCC to treat the data to be serialized as an array of the child type instead of as a scalar.

subject: func_ty parameter/attribute
truth: Every usage of func_ty has been replaced with prototype; use prototype instead of func_ty everywhere.
quote: Every usage of the name func_ty has been replaced with the name prototype.

subject: ANA serialization backend
truth: Stop using ANA for serialization; use pickle instead, or angr.vaults for identity-aware use cases.
quote: ANA, angr's old identity-aware serialization backend, has been removed. Instead of non-obvious serialization behavior, all angr objects should now be pickleable.

subject: IDA backend for CLE
truth: Stop using the IDA backend for CLE; it has been removed.
quote: The IDA backend for CLE has been removed. It has been broken for quite some time, but now it has been disabled for your own safety.

subject: Surveyors
truth: Stop using Surveyors; they have been removed. Use the Exploration Technique for Slicecutor instead.
quote: Surveyors have been removed! Finally! This is thanks to @danse-macabre who contributed an Exploration Technique for the Slicecutor.

subject: UnresolvableTarget exception
truth: Stop catching UnresolvableTarget; catch UnresolvableJumpTarget or UnresolvableCallTarget instead.
quote: UnresolvableTarget is now split into two classes: UnresolvableJumpTarget and UnresolvableCallTarget.

subject: Simulation Manager save_unconstrained flag
new: save_unconstrained flag now on by default
truth: The save_unconstrained flag of Simulation Manager is now on by default, which may cause performance changes or unexpected state retention.
quote: The save_unconstrained flag of Simulation Manager is now on by default

from_fact: save_unconstrained flag now on by default

subject: Simulation Manager keyword argument validation
old: Simulation Manager silently ignored incorrect keyword arguments
new: Simulation Manager throws an error on incorrect keyword arguments
truth: Simulation Manager now throws an error if you pass incorrect keyword arguments, instead of silently ignoring them.
quote: Simulation Manager will throw an error if you pass incorrect keyword arguments (??? why was it like this)

from_fact: simulation manager silently ignored incorrect keyword arguments
simulation manager throws an error on incorrect keyword arguments

subject: unsatisfiable state handling in simulation steps
old: unsatisfiable states only appeared in 'unsat' stash when save_unsat was set
new: unsatisfiable states always appear in 'unsat' stash regardless of save_unsat setting
truth: Unsatisfiable states now always appear in the 'unsat' stash regardless of the save_unsat setting; add 'unsat' to auto_drop to restore old behavior.
quote: If a step produces only unsatisfiable states, they will appear in the 'unsat' stash regardless of the save_unsat setting, since this usually indicates a bug. Add unsat to the auto_drop parameter to restore the old behavior.

from_fact: unsatisfiable states only appeared in 'unsat' stash when save_unsat was set
unsatisfiable states always appear in 'unsat' stash regardless of save_unsat setting

subject: object.symbols_by_addr
truth: Stop using object.symbols_by_addr (dict); it has been removed. Use object.symbols (sorted list) instead.
quote: Remove object.symbols_by_addr (dict) and add object.symbols (sorted list); add fuzzy parameter to loader.find_symbol

subject: CFGAccurate
truth: Stop using CFGAccurate; it has been renamed to CFGEmulated.
quote: CFGAccurate has been renamed to CFGEmulated.

subject: LoopLimiter
truth: LoopLimiter has been removed and is no longer available.
quote: Remove LoopLimiter and DFG.

subject: DFG
truth: DFG has been removed and is no longer available.
quote: Remove LoopLimiter and DFG.

subject: state.options type
old: state.options was a set of strings
new: state.options is now a SimStateOptions instance
truth: state.options is no longer a set of strings; it is now an instance of SimStateOptions.
quote: state.options is no longer a set of strings.

from_fact: state.options was a set of strings
state.options is now a simstateoptions instance

subject: project._simos
truth: Stop accessing project._simos; use project.simos instead.
quote: The SimOS for a project is now a public property - project.simos instead of project._simos.

subject: LAZY_SOLVES default
truth: LAZY_SOLVES is no longer enabled by default during symbolic execution; you must explicitly enable it if needed.
quote: SimuVEX: LAZY_SOLVES is no longer enabled by default during symbolic execution. It's still there if it's wanted, but it just caused confusion when on by default.

subject: translation and execution engine architecture
old: angr bound to VEX IR for lifting native code
truth: angr's code lifting is no longer bound solely to VEX; a new pluggable engine paradigm has replaced the old VEX-bound architecture.
quote: Until now, angr has been bound to the VEX intermediate representation to lift native code, supporting a wide range of architectures but not being very expandable past them.

from_fact: angr bound to vex ir for lifting native code

subject: SimRun class
old: SimRun class with subclasses instantiated from an input state
truth: Stop using SimRun; engines now have a process() function that produces a SimSuccessors instance.
quote: the SimRun class has been eliminated. Instead of different subclasses of SimRun that would be instantiated from an input state, engines each have a process function that, from an input state, produces a SimSuccessors instance

from_fact: simrun class with subclasses instantiated from an input state

subject: state.mem[x:] assignment
truth: state.mem[x:] = y now requires a type for storage, e.g. state.mem[x:].dword = y.
quote: state.mem[x:] = y now requires a type for storage (for example state.mem[x:].dword = y).

subject: calling inline SimProcedures
old: old way of calling inline SimProcedures
new: create a SimProcedure, then call execute() on it with a program state and arguments
truth: Stop using the old inline SimProcedure calling convention; now you must create a SimProcedure and call execute() on it passing a program state and arguments.
quote: the way of calling inline SimProcedures has been changed. Now you have to create a SimProcedure, and then call execute() on it and pass in a program state as well as the arguments.

from_fact: old way of calling inline simprocedures
create a simprocedure, then call execute() on it with a program state and arguments

subject: accessing registers without triggering breakpoints/actions
old: state.regs.eax (always triggers SimInspect breakpoints and creates actions)
new: state.regs._eax or state._ip (prefix with underscore to avoid breakpoints/actions)
truth: Accessing registers via state.regs.eax always triggers breakpoints and actions; prefix with underscore (state.regs._eax) to avoid them.
quote: accessing registers through SimRegNameView (like state.regs.eax) always triggers SimInspect breakpoints and creates new actions. Now you can access a register by prefixing its name with an underscore (e.g. state.regs._eax or state._ip) to avoid triggering breakpoints or creating actions.

from_fact: state.regs.eax (always triggers siminspect breakpoints and creates actions)
state.regs._eax or state._ip (prefix with underscore to avoid breakpoints/actions)

subject: max_size parameter in angr.Block constructor
truth: Stop using max_size; use size instead in angr.Block constructor and upstream methods like project.factory.block, path.step, path_group.step.
quote: we have deprecated the keyword argument max_size and changed it to to size in the angr.Block constructor (i.e., the argument to project.factory.block and more upstream methods (path.step, path_group.step, etc).

subject: project.factory.sim_run
truth: Stop using project.factory.sim_run; use project.factory.successors instead, which now generates a SimSuccessors object.
quote: we have deprecated project.factory.sim_run and changed it to to project.factory.successors, and it now generates a SimSuccessors object.

subject: project.factory.sim_block
truth: Stop using project.factory.sim_block; use project.factory.successors(default_engine=True) instead.
quote: project.factory.sim_block has been deprecated and replaced with project.factory.successors(default_engine=True).

subject: syscall detection via project.is_hooked
old: project.is_hooked(addr) to check for syscall SimProcedure
truth: Syscalls are no longer hooks; stop relying on project.is_hooked(addr) for syscalls, check project._simos.syscall_table.get_by_addr(addr) instead.
quote: If you were using project.is_hooked(addr) to see if an address has a related SimProcedure, now you probably want to check if there is a related syscall as well (using project._simos.syscall_table.get_by_addr(addr) is not

from_fact: project.is_hooked(addr) to check for syscall simprocedure

subject: BackwardSlicing
truth: Stop using BackwardSlicing; it is temporarily disabled and will be re-enabled once DDG-related refactors are merged.
quote: as part of the refactor, BackwardSlicing is temporarily disabled. It will be re-enabled once all DDG-related refactor are merged to master.

subject: Labels storage
new: Labels are now stored in KnowledgeBase
truth: Labels are now stored in KnowledgeBase instead of their previous location; code accessing labels from the old location will break.
quote: Labels are now stored in KnowledgeBase.

from_fact: labels are now stored in knowledgebase

subject: Region mapping location
truth: Region mapping has been moved from SimAbstractMemory to SimMemory; code referencing SimAbstractMemory for region mapping will break.
quote: Move region mapping from SimAbstractMemory to SimMemory.

subject: support_selfmodifying_code project option
old: allowed state as fallback source of instruction bytes when no CLE backer available
new: makes instruction lifting use the state as the source of bytes always
truth: The support_selfmodifying_code option now always uses the state as the source of instruction bytes, not just as a fallback.
quote: The behavior of the support_selfmodifying_code project option is changed: Before, this would allow the state to be used as a fallback source of instruction bytes when no backer from CLE is available. Now, this option makes instruction lifting use the state as the source of bytes always.

from_fact: allowed state as fallback source of instruction bytes when no cle backer available
makes instruction lifting use the state as the source of bytes always

subject: syscall handling
old: syscalls produced a SimRun for a syscall helper
new: syscalls produce a SimProcedure for the given syscall, each syscall has its own address in a syscalls segment, and syscalls are treated as jumps to this segment
truth: Syscalls now produce a SimProcedure instead of a SimRun, have their own address in a syscalls segment, and are treated as jumps to this segment.
quote: Syscalls are handled differently now - Before you would see a SimRun for a syscall helper, now you'll just see a SimProcedure for the given syscall. Additionally, each syscall has its own address in a "syscalls segment", and syscalls are treated as jumps to this segment.

from_fact: syscalls produced a simrun for a syscall helper
syscalls produce a simprocedure for the given syscall, each syscall has its own address in a syscalls segment, and syscalls are treated as jumps to this segment

subject: syscall handling
truth: Stop using simuvex.procedures.syscalls.handler; use angr.SimOS.handle_syscall() instead.
quote: Syscalls are no longer handled by simuvex.procedures.syscalls.handler. Instead, syscalls are now handled by angr.SimOS.handle_syscall().

subject: syscall SimProcedure address
old: syscall SimProcedure address was the address right after the syscall instruction
new: each syscall SimProcedure has its own address
truth: Syscall SimProcedure addresses are no longer the address right after the syscall instruction; each now has its own unique address, which may break code that relied on the old address mapping.
quote: Previously, the address of a syscall SimProcedure is the address right after the syscall instruction (e.g. int 80h), which collides with the real basic block starting at that address, and is very confusing. Now each syscall SimProcedure has its own address, just as a normal SimProcedure.

from_fact: syscall simprocedure address was the address right after the syscall instruction
each syscall simprocedure has its own address

subject: Claripy division operators
new: __div__ now performs signed division; use SDiv for unsigned division
truth: Claripy's __div__ no longer does unsigned division; use SDiv for signed division and SMod for signed remainder.
quote: Fixed a bug in claripy where __div__ was not always doing unsigned division, and added new methods SDiv and SMod for signed division and signed remainder, respectively.

from_fact: __div__ now performs signed division; use sdiv for unsigned division

subject: Claripy solver frontends
truth: Claripy frontends have been completely rewritten into a mixin-centric design; custom frontend code will break.
quote: Claripy frontends have been completely rewritten into a mixin-centric solver design.

subject: SimuVEX default solver
truth: SimuVEX now uses the composite solver by default, which may cause behavioral differences during symbolic execution.
quote: With the recent changes to Claripy, we have configured SimuVEX to use the composite solver by default. This should be transparent, but should be considered if strange issues (or differences in behavior) arise during symbolic execution.

subject: CFG
truth: CFG is now an alias to CFGFast instead of CFGAccurate; use CFGAccurate explicitly if you need context-sensitivity or state-preserving features.
quote: CFG is now an alias to CFGFast instead of CFGAccurate.

subject: PyVEX submodules IRExpr, IRStmt, IRConst
truth: PyVEX modules IRExpr, IRStmt, and IRConst no longer exist; use pyvex.expr, pyvex.stmt, and pyvex.const instead.
quote: The IRExpr, IRStmt, and IRConst modules no longer exist as submodules, and those module names are deprecated.

subject: pyvex.IRSB keyword parameter names
old: old keyword parameter names for IRSB
new: new keyword parameter names (data, mem_addr, arch)
truth: The first three required parameters to pyvex.IRSB have been renamed; switch to positional args or use the new keyword names data, mem_addr, arch.
quote: The names of the first three parameters to pyvex.IRSB (the required ones) have been changed.

from_fact: old keyword parameter names for irsb
new keyword parameter names (data, mem_addr, arch)

subject: sargc parameter in entry_state/full_init_state
truth: The sargc parameter to entry_state and full_init_state has been removed; use the new argc parameter instead.
quote: The optional parameter sargc to the entry_state and full_init_state constructors has been removed and replaced with an argc parameter.

subject: claripy.backend_concrete
truth: Stop using claripy.backend_concrete, use claripy.backends.concrete instead.
quote: claripy.backend_concrete is now claripy.backends.concrete, claripy.backend_vsa is now claripy.backends.vsa, and so on.

subject: claripy.backend_vsa
truth: Stop using claripy.backend_vsa, use claripy.backends.vsa instead.
quote: claripy.backend_concrete is now claripy.backends.concrete, claripy.backend_vsa is now claripy.backends.vsa, and so on.

subject: claripy.BV
truth: Stop using claripy.BV, use claripy.BVS (bit-vector symbol) instead.
quote: claripy.BV has been renamed to claripy.BVS (bit-vector symbol).

subject: BV.model
truth: Stop using BV.model, convert with the appropriate backend instead (e.g. claripy.backend_concrete.convert(bv)).
quote: BV.model is deprecated. If you're using it, you're doing something wrong, anyways. If you really need a specific model, convert it with the appropriate backend (i.e., claripy.backend_concrete.convert(bv)).

subject: CFG argument keep_input_state
truth: Stop using keep_input_state argument for CFG, use keep_state instead (which keeps both input and final states).
quote: CFG argument keep_input_state has been renamed to keep_state. With this option enabled, both input and final states are kept.

subject: BackwardSlicing arguments cfg_node and stmt_id
truth: Stop using cfg_node and stmt_id arguments for BackwardSlicing, use the single targets argument instead (supports slicing from multiple sources).
quote: Two arguments cfg_node and stmt_id of BackwardSlicing have been deprecated. Instead, BackwardSlicing takes a single argument, targets.

subject: StringSpec
truth: Stop using StringSpec, pass claripy bitvectors directly as arguments instead.
quote: StringSpec is deprecated! You can now pass claripy bitvectors directly as arguments.
